title: Mito-magnolol compounds and methods of synthesis and use thereof
abstract: The present invention provides mito-magnolol compounds, pharmaceutical compositions thereof, and methods of using the mito-magnolol compounds in the treatment of cancer, especially anti-cancer therapy or kinase resistant cancers.
claims: Claims Claims  We Claim: 1. A mito-magnolol compound of formula (I) wherein X is selected from a Ci-Cis alkyl, phenyl and polyethylene glycol (PEG); Each Y is independently selected from -H, -CF3, methyl (Me), Cl, OMe, C(0)CEE, NO2, N(Me) 2 ; and R is selected from H, Ci-Cis alkyl, phenyl, benzyl and polyethylene glycol (PEG). 2 The mito-magnolol compound of claim 1, wherein X is Ci-Cisalkyl, each Y is H and R is H. 3. The mito-magnolol compound of claim 2, wherein the compound is wherein n is an integer between 1 and 18. 4. The mito-magnolol compound of claim 3, wherein the compound is . 5. The mito-magnolol compound of claim 1, wherein the compound is: 6. The mito-magnolol compound of claim 1, wherein the compound is: 7. The mito-magnolol compound of claim 1, wherein the compound is: (i) (ii) 8. A mito-magnolol compound of formula (II) wherein each n is independently selected from an integer from 1-18. 9. The mito-magnolol compound of claim 8, wherein the compound is 10. A composition comprising the mito-magnolol compound of any one of claims 1-9 and a pharmaceutically acceptable carrier. 11. A method of treating cancer in a subject having cancer comprising: administering the mito-magnolol compound of any one of claims 1-9 in a therapeutically effective amount to treat the cancer. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the cancer is melanoma. 13. The method of claim 11 or 12, wherein the cancer is resistant to anti-cancer therapy. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein the anti-cancer therapy is a BRAF inhibitor. 15. The method of claim 13, wherein the anti-cancer therapy is a checkpoint inhibitor. 16. The method of any one of claims 11-15, wherein the mito-magnolol compound is administered in combination with one or more additional anti-cancer therapies. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein the anti-cancer therapy is a BRAF inhibitor. 18. A method of reducing or inhibiting cancer cell growth in a subject having cancer, the method comprising: administering the mito-magnolol compound of any one of claims 1-9 in a therapeutically effective amount to reduce or inhibit cancer cell growth. 19. The method of claim 18, wherein the mito-magnolol compound is administered in combination with one or more anti-cancer therapies. 20. A method of preventing or delaying resistance of a cancer to an anti-cancer therapy in a subject, the method comprising: administering the mito-magnolol compound of any one of claims 1-9 in a therapeutically effective amount to prevent or delay resistance of the cancer to the anti-cancer therapy. 21. The method of claim 20, wherein the mito-magnolol compound is administered in combination with an anti-cancer therapy. 22. The method of claim 21, wherein the anti-cancer therapy is co-currently administered. 23. A method of increasing a T cell response to an anti-cancer therapy in a cancer patient, the method comprising administering the mito-magnolol compound of any one of claims 1-9 in a therapeutically effective amount to increase the T cell response to the anti-cancer therapy. 24. The method of claim 23, wherein the anti-cancer therapy is an inhibitor of an oncogenic kinase. 25. The method of claim 23, wherein the anti-cancer therapy is a checkpoint inhibitor. 26. The method of any one of claims 23-25, wherein the mito-magnolol compound is administered in combination with one or more anti-cancer therapies 27. A kit comprising at least one mito-magnolol compound of claim 1-9, a pharmaceutically acceptable carrier or diluent, and instructional material.
